option.name: text
option.value: - Use camelCase for function and variable names. - Use PascalCase for class and enum names. - Prefix interface names with a capital `I` (e.g., `IRepository`) unless they are functional interfaces. - Use `sealed class` for representing UI states and events. - Use `enum class` for fixed sets of options like `Frequency` or `RecurrenceType`. - Use `when` expressions instead of cascading `if-else` when handling multiple branches. - Use `!!` only when you are certain a value is non-null; prefer safe calls or `?.let`. - Use `takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }` to skip empty strings in form submissions. - Write KDoc comments for all public functions, classes, and properties explaining their purpose and usage. - Use `@Suppress("unused")` for intentionally unused functions (e.g., for future features). - Ensure all imports are optimized (remove unused) before committing. - Implement Repository pattern: create a `BudgetRepository` class that abstracts data sources (Room). - Use `StateFlow` or `LiveData` in ViewModels to expose data to UI; prefer `StateFlow` for coroutines integration. - Use `private val` for ViewModel state flows, expose immutable `StateFlow` via public property. - Use `MutableStateFlow` with `private set` and expose immutable `StateFlow` in ViewModel. - In `onCreate`, set up observers after initializing views and repository. - Use `by viewModels` delegate with a custom `ViewModelProvider.Factory` for ViewModel instantiation with dependencies. - Use `by viewModels` with a factory to inject repository into ViewModel. - For temporary budget IDs, use a dummy value (e.g., `1L`) until proper month selection is implemented. - Always use `viewModelScope.launch` for coroutines in ViewModels. - Use `launch` inside `viewModelScope` for coroutines; avoid using `runBlocking`. - Collect flows in `lifecycleScope.launch` within Activities/Fragments; never call `collect` directly in lifecycle methods without a coroutine scope. - When observing flows, use `lifecycleScope.launch` and `collect` within a coroutine. - In `updateCategoriesUI`, use `lifecycleScope.launch` inside the loop to collect expense list for each category. - Name layout files in snake_case corresponding to the screen/component (e.g., `activity_income_expenses.xml`, `item_income_row.xml`). - Use `ViewBinding` for type-safe access to views; inflate in `onCreate` and store in a `lateinit var`. - Use `isVisible` extension from `androidx.core.view` for visibility toggling. - Replace `paddingStart`/`paddingEnd` with `paddingHorizontal` for RTL support. - Set `maxWidth="488dp"` on text fields in dialogs to adhere to Material Design guidelines. - For dynamic UI elements like income rows, use a ViewHolder pattern and store state in `tag`. - When building dynamic views, inflate layouts and use `removeAllViews()` before repopulating. - For dialogs, create a custom theme extending `Theme.AppCompat.Light.Dialog.Alert` to apply consistent styling. - Validate user input in dialogs by disabling positive buttons until valid input is entered using `TextWatcher`. - Set button text programmatically using `getString(R.string.id)` with placeholders when needed. - Use `Toast.makeText().show()` for user feedback after successful saves. - Use `Toast.LENGTH_SHORT` for brief messages; avoid long toasts. - For delete confirmation dialogs, use `setMessage` with placeholder string and `getString` with arguments. - Use `android.R.id.message` to access dialog message TextView for custom font if needed. - When implementing `TextWatcher`, override all three methods; use `onTextChanged` for enabling/disabling buttons. - Annotate Room entities with `@Entity`, primary keys with `@PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true)`. - Use `@Dao` interfaces with suspend functions for database operations. - Write `@Database` class with abstract DAO methods and a `companion object` with `getDatabase()` singleton. - Use `ksp` instead of `kapt` for Room annotation processing. - Use `suspend` functions for all database insert/update/delete operations. - Use `Flow` for database queries that return multiple values. - In `BudgetRepository`, delegate each DAO call to the corresponding DAO method. - Use `try-catch` around database operations that may throw, and log errors with `Log.e()` (remove before production). - In `Expense` entity, use `recurrenceType: RecurrenceType` and `recurrenceInterval: Int?` for recurring expenses. - For date pickers, store timestamps (milliseconds) in database; convert to `Date` or `Calendar` for display. - Format dates with `SimpleDateFormat("MM/dd/yyyy", Locale.US)` for display in expense items. - Use `Date(expense.dueDate)` to convert timestamp to Date object for formatting. - Use `SimpleDateFormat` with explicit `Locale` to avoid locale-sensitive issues. - Define string resources in `strings.xml`, color resources in `colors.xml`, and dimension resources in `dimens.xml`. - Use `@ColorRes` and `@StringRes` annotations when referencing resources in code. - Use `@ColorInt` annotation when passing color integers to methods that expect color values. - Use `toColorInt()` from `androidx.core.graphics` to parse color strings. - All string literals must be extracted to `strings.xml`; avoid hardcoding. - Format currency amounts using `String.format(Locale.US, "%.2f", amount)` and concatenate with currency symbol using string resources. - For fonts, create a `font` resource directory and reference fonts via `@font/filename`. - Use `style="@style/TextAppearance.BudgetBrewer.Header"` in TextViews to apply font styles consistently. - Override dialog title font programmatically using `ResourcesCompat.getFont()` in `setOnShowListener` for pre-26 compatibility. - Ensure `minSdk = 24` and use `ResourcesCompat.getFont()` for loading fonts to maintain compatibility. - Define custom styles for buttons, text appearances, and alert dialogs in `themes.xml`. - Use `android:textAppearance` for applying styles to buttons while preserving button defaults. - For custom button style, define `Widget.BudgetBrewer.Button` and set `android:backgroundTint` and `android:textColor`. - Use `app:tint` on `ImageButton` for compatibility; set to `#212121` for contrast. - Always add `contentDescription` to ImageButtons and ImageViews for accessibility. - Add `autofillHints` to EditText fields for accessibility. - In expense dialogs, initialize selected date variable and enable positive button only after date is chosen. - For recurrence options, store `recurrenceType` as enum and `recurrenceInterval` as nullable Int. - Use `when` to handle recurrence type when populating edit dialog. - Clear input fields after deletion or when switching to editable mode. - For dynamic income rows, store `weekNumber` in the holder to match with existing income. - In `saveIncomeRows`, iterate over all rows and only process those in editable mode. - When updating an existing income, use `existing.copy(sourceName = source, amount = amount)` to preserve id and currency. - When inserting new income, pass `weekNumber` to ViewModel. - After saving, update the read-only text views with new values and switch visibility. - Use `updateSaveButtonState()` to enable/disable save button based on presence of editable rows. - Use `filter` to get expenses for a specific category in `updateCategoriesUI`. - Set `android:exported="true"` only for launcher activities; other activities should have `exported="false"` unless needed. - In `AndroidManifest.xml`, declare all activities with proper `android:name` and set `exported` appropriately. - Disable built-in Kotlin in `gradle.properties` if using AGP 8.x with `android.builtInKotlin=false` and `android.newDsl=false` for stability. - Keep all project dependencies in `libs.versions.toml` and use version catalogs. - Follow the standard Android project structure: `data/`, `database/`, `ui/`, `ui/incomeexpenses/`. - Place data models (entities) in `data` package, DAO and database classes in `database` package.
